question:
If your engine runs a pump used during delivery of compressed gas, should you turn off the engine before or after unhooking hoses after delivery?
1) Leave it running until you leave
2) Turn it off after product transfer, before you unhook the hose
3) None of these
4) Turn it off before product transfer, before you hook the hose
5) Turn it off during product transfer, while unhooking the hose
canonical_answer: Turn it off after product transfer, before you unhook the hose

appears_in.title: Hazmat Endorsement Nj
appears_in.answer: Turn it off after product transfer, before you unhook the hose

appears_in.title: Hazmat Practice Test Nj
appears_in.answer: Turn it off after product transfer, before you unhook the hose

appears_in.title: Hazmat Test Nj
appears_in.answer: Turn it off after product transfer, before you unhook the hose

appears_in.title: Nj Hazmat Practice Test
appears_in.answer: Turn it off after product transfer, before you unhook the hose

appears_in.title: Nj Hazmat Test Answers
appears_in.answer: Turn it off after product transfer, before you unhook the hose
